```{r,warning=FALSE}
  sden <- data.frame(bri.hyper.sd(model$marginals.hyperpar[[1]]))
  hyperparam_plot = ggplot(sden,aes(x,y)) + geom_line() + ylab("density") + 
             xlab("linear predictor")
  
  #Store the results in the list
  kgr_model5_results = list(
    K_AR_heatmap = K_AR_heatmap, 
    K_DL_heatmap = K_DL_heatmap,
    K_Interaction_heatmap = K_Interaction_heatmap,
    K_AR_weight = K_AR_norm / (K_AR_norm + K_DL_norm + K_Int_norm),
    K_DL_weight = K_DL_norm / (K_AR_norm + K_DL_norm + K_Int_norm),
    K_Int_weight = K_Int_norm / (K_AR_norm + K_DL_norm + K_Int_norm),
    K_weight = K_weight/(K_weight + gfilter_weight),
    gfilter_weight = gfilter_weight/(K_weight + gfilter_weight),
    covmatrix = covGP5,
    prec = inv_covGP5,
    num_jitters = covGP_jittered[[2]],
    prec_heatmap = inv_covGP5_heatmap,
    model_summary = model_summary,
    bri_hyperpar_summary = bri_hyperpar_summary,
    exp_effects = multeff,
    param_plot = param_plot,
    hyperparam_plot = hyperparam_plot,
    model_DIC = model_DIC,
    model_WAIC = model_WAIC,
    fitted_values = preds_model,
    marg_fitted_values = marginal_fvs
  )
  
  return(kgr_model5_results)
}

#Fit kgr_model5
kgr_model5_fit = kgr_model5(dataset = inla_train_dataset, rho_AR_rbf = 1.019648e-04, 
                            rho_AR_periodic = 3.904021e-04, rho_DL_rbf = 6.926401e-04,
                            rho_DL_periodic = 1.378534e-05, rho_int_rbf = 1.494036e-04,
                            rho_int_periodic = 3.228066e-04, sigma2_AR = 5.567759e-01,
                            sigma2_DL = 5.955738e-01, sigma2_int = 2.712031, link=1)

#Extract DIC and WAIC 
kgr_model5_DIC = kgr_model5_fit$model_DIC
kgr_model5_WAIC = kgr_model5_fit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model5_fit$model_summary
kgr_model5_fit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model5_fit$exp_effects
kgr_model5_fit$K_AR_weight
kgr_model5_fit$K_DL_weight
kgr_model5_fit$K_Int_weight
kgr_model5_fit$K_weight
kgr_model5_fit$gfilter_weight
kgr_model5_fit$num_jitters

#Show plots
kgr_model5_fit$K_AR_heatmap
kgr_model5_fit$K_DL_heatmap
kgr_model5_fit$K_Interaction_heatmap
kgr_model5_fit$prec_heatmap
kgr_model5_fit$param_plot
kgr_model5_fit$hyperparam_plot
pp_insample_plot(pred_data = kgr_model5_fit$fitted_values)
```
Fit some nonlinear models: 

```{r,eval=FALSE}
m.poly = inla(mpg ~ hp + I(hp^2) + I(hp^3), 
              data = test, control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE))
m.poly$summary.fixed
fvs.poly = m.poly$summary.fitted.values

ggplot(test,aes(x=hp,y=mpg)) + 
        geom_point() + geom_line(aes(y=fvs.poly$mean),color = "red") + 
        geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = fvs.poly$`0.025quant`,
                        ymax = fvs.poly$`0.975quant`),alpha = 0.3) 



knots = seq(50,350,length.out=3)
m.bsplines = inla(mpg ~ bs(hp,knots = knots),
                 data = test, control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE))
m.bsplines$summary.fixed
fvs.bsplines = m.bsplines$summary.fitted.values

ggplot(test,aes(x=hp,y=mpg)) + 
        geom_point() + geom_line(aes(y=fvs.bsplines$mean),color = "red") + 
        geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = fvs.bsplines$`0.025quant`,
                        ymax = fvs.bsplines$`0.975quant`),alpha = 0.3) 



m.nsplines = inla(mpg ~ ns(hp,df=3),
                 data = test, control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE))
m.nsplines$summary.fixed
fvs.nsplines = m.nsplines$summary.fitted.values

ggplot(test,aes(x=hp,y=mpg)) + 
        geom_point() + geom_line(aes(y=fvs.nsplines$mean),color = "red") + 
        geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = fvs.nsplines$`0.025quant`,
                        ymax = fvs.nsplines$`0.975quant`),alpha = 0.3) 
```

We can add splines to our log intensity function pretty easily 


# Fit models out of sample:

## BYM model

```{r}
#Run model 
ref_model2_outfit = ref_model2(dataset = inla_dataset_toimpute,a_prec_prior = 1,b_prec_prior = 5e-4,a_phi_prior = 1,b_phi_prior = 5e-4)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
ref_model2_DIC = ref_model2_outfit$model_DIC
ref_model2_WAIC = ref_model2_outfit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
ref_model2_outfit$model_summary %>% kable %>% kable_styling()
ref_model2_outfit$bri_hyperpar_summary %>% kable %>% kable_styling()
ref_model2_outfit$exp_effects

#Show plots
ref_model2_outfit$param_plot
ref_model2_outfit$hyperparam_plot
pp_outsample_plot(ref_data = inla_dataset,
                  pred_data = ref_model2_outfit$fitted_values)
```

## KGR model 1

```{r}
#Fit kgr_model1
kgr_model1_outfit = kgr_model1(dataset = inla_dataset_toimpute,rho_time_rbf = 536.519, rho_time_periodic = 5.707,sigma2_time = 2.773)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model1_DIC = kgr_model1_outfit$model_DIC
kgr_model1_WAIC = kgr_model1_outfit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model1_outfit$model_summary
kgr_model1_outfit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model1_outfit$exp_effects
kgr_model1_outfit$K_time_weight
kgr_model1_outfit$gfilter_weight

#Show plots
kgr_model1_outfit$K_time_heatmap
kgr_model1_outfit$param_plot
kgr_model1_outfit$hyperparam_plot
pp_outsample_plot(ref_data = inla_dataset,
                  pred_data = kgr_model1_outfit$fitted_values)
```

## KGR model 2

```{r}
#Fit kgr_model1
kgr_model2_outfit = kgr_model2(dataset = inla_dataset_toimpute,
                               rho_Z_rbf = 0.0020604943, rho_Z_periodic = 0.0024283451,
                               sigma2_Z = 3.941096, link=1)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model2_DIC = kgr_model2_outfit$model_DIC
kgr_model2_WAIC = kgr_model2_outfit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model2_outfit$model_summary
kgr_model2_outfit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model2_outfit$exp_effects
kgr_model2_outfit$K_time_weight
kgr_model2_outfit$gfilter_weight

#Show plots
kgr_model2_outfit$K_time_heatmap
kgr_model2_outfit$param_plot
kgr_model2_outfit$hyperparam_plot
pp_outsample_plot(ref_data = inla_dataset,
                  pred_data = kgr_model2_outfit$fitted_values)
```

## KGR model 3

```{r,warning=FALSE}
#Fit kgr_model3
kgr_model3_outfit = kgr_model3(dataset = inla_dataset_toimpute, 
                            rho_Z_rbf = 37801.48, 
                            rho_Z_periodic = 571512.56,
                            rho_time_rbf = 39.5276, 
                            rho_time_periodic = 380794.8,
                            sigma2 = 1.124888)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model3_DIC = kgr_model3_outfit$model_DIC
kgr_model3_WAIC = kgr_model3_outfit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model3_outfit$model_summary
kgr_model3_outfit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model3_outfit$exp_effects
kgr_model3_outfit$K_weight
kgr_model3_outfit$gfilter_weight

#Show plots
kgr_model3_outfit$K_time_heatmap
kgr_model3_outfit$K_Z_heatmap
kgr_model3_outfit$param_plot
kgr_model3_outfit$hyperparam_plot
pp_outsample_plot(ref_data = inla_dataset,
                  pred_data = kgr_model3_outfit$fitted_values)
```

## KGR model 4

```{r,warning=FALSE}
#Fit kgr_model3
kgr_model4_outfit = kgr_model4(dataset = inla_dataset_toimpute, 
                            rho_Z_rbf = 0.0008842497,
                            rho_Z_periodic = 0.0012119621,
                            rho_time_rbf = 0.0037851329,
                            rho_time_periodic = 0.003501742,
                            sigma2_Z = 4.310359,
                            sigma2_time = 0.9296752, link=1)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model4_DIC = kgr_model4_outfit$model_DIC
kgr_model4_WAIC = kgr_model4_outfit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model4_outfit$model_summary
kgr_model4_outfit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model4_outfit$exp_effects
kgr_model4_outfit$K_weight
kgr_model4_outfit$gfilter_weight

#Show plots
kgr_model4_outfit$K_time_heatmap
kgr_model4_outfit$K_Z_heatmap
kgr_model4_outfit$param_plot
kgr_model4_outfit$hyperparam_plot
pp_outsample_plot(ref_data = inla_dataset,
                  pred_data = kgr_model4_outfit$fitted_values)
```

## KGR model 5

```{r,warning=FALSE}
#Fit kgr_model5
kgr_model5_outfit = kgr_model5(dataset = inla_dataset_toimpute, rho_AR_rbf = 1.019648e-04, 
                            rho_AR_periodic = 3.904021e-04, rho_DL_rbf = 6.926401e-04,
                            rho_DL_periodic = 1.378534e-05, rho_int_rbf = 1.494036e-04,
                            rho_int_periodic = 3.228066e-04, sigma2_AR = 5.567759e-01,
                            sigma2_DL = 5.955738e-01, sigma2_int = 2.712031, link=1)

#Extract DIC and WAIC
kgr_model5_DIC = kgr_model5_outfit$model_DIC
kgr_model5_WAIC = kgr_model5_outfit$model_WAIC

#Get summaries of parameter estimates
kgr_model5_outfit$model_summary
kgr_model5_outfit$bri_hyperpar_summary
kgr_model5_outfit$exp_effects
kgr_model5_outfit$K_AR_weight
kgr_model5_outfit$K_DL_weight
kgr_model5_outfit$K_Int_weight
kgr_model5_outfit$K_weight
kgr_model5_outfit$gfilter_weight
kgr_model5_outfit$num_jitters

#Show plots
kgr_model5_outfit$K_AR_heatmap
kgr_model5_outfit$K_DL_heatmap
kgr_model5_outfit$K_Interaction_heatmap
kgr_model5_outfit$prec_heatmap
kgr_model5_outfit$param_plot
kgr_model5_outfit$hyperparam_plot
pp_outsample_plot(ref_data = inla_dataset,
                  pred_data = kgr_model5_outfit$fitted_values)
```

# Evaluating model fit

## Comparing out of sample MAE, MASE, and MAPE for different clusters between models

```{r}
print(degree_connectivity)
```

Mean absolute error (MAE) is a measure of the average size of the mistakes in a collection of predictions, without taking their direction into account

MAE = $\frac{1}{h_{max}} \sum_{h=1}^{h_{max}} | \hat \lambda_{t+h}^{obs} - \hat \lambda_{t+h}|$ where $\hat \lambda_{t+h}^{obs}$ is the average of the number of deaths observed for month $t+h$ over all years

```{r}
MAE_table = matrix(nrow=num_counties,ncol=6)

for (i in 1:num_counties){
  actual = inla_dataset %>% filter(county_id == i)  %>% data.frame()

  pm_1 = ref_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_2 = kgr_model1_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_3 = kgr_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_4 = kgr_model3_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_5 = kgr_model4_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_6 = kgr_model5_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()

  actual_test = c()
  
  for (j in 1:12){
    actual_j = actual %>% filter(month == j) %>% select(deaths)
    est_lambda = mean(actual_j$deaths)
    actual_test = c(actual_test,as.numeric(est_lambda))
  }
  
  actual_test = c(actual_test[5:12],actual_test)

  pm_1_test = pm_1[50:69,]
  pm_2_test = pm_2[50:69,]
  pm_3_test = pm_3[50:69,]
  pm_4_test = pm_4[50:69,]
  pm_5_test = pm_5[50:69,]
  pm_6_test = pm_6[50:69,]

  actual_test_mean = mean(actual_test)

  mae1 = mean(abs(actual_test - pm_1_test$mean))
  mae2 = mean(abs(actual_test - pm_2_test$mean))
  mae3 = mean(abs(actual_test - pm_3_test$mean))
  mae4 = mean(abs(actual_test - pm_4_test$mean))
  mae5 = mean(abs(actual_test - pm_5_test$mean))
  mae6 = mean(abs(actual_test - pm_6_test$mean))
  
  MAE_table[i,] = c(mae1,mae2,mae3,mae4,mae5,mae6)
  # MAE_table[,i] = MAE_table[,i] / actual_test_mean
}

#Table 2: MAE on test dataset

MAE_table = data.frame(MAE_table)

rownames(MAE_table) = clusterlabels$counties
colnames(MAE_table) = c("BYM model","Proposed KGR model 1","Proposed KGR model 2","Proposed KGR model 3","Proposed KGR model 4","Proposed KGR model 5")

MAE_table
```

Mean absolute scaled error (MASE) is a measure of the accuracy of forecasts. It is the mean absolute error of the forecast values, divided by the mean absolute error of the in-sample one-step naive forecast.

MASE = $\frac{\frac{1}{n} \sum_{t=1}^n |\hat \lambda_{t+h}^{obs} - \hat \lambda_{t+h}|}{\frac{1}{n-1} \sum_{t=2}^n |\hat \lambda_{t+h}^{obs} - \hat \lambda_{t+h-1}^{obs}|}$

```{r}
MASE_table = matrix(nrow=num_counties,ncol=6)

for (i in 1:num_counties){
  actual = inla_dataset %>% filter(county_id == i)  %>% data.frame()

  pm_1 = ref_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_2 = kgr_model1_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_3 = kgr_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_4 = kgr_model3_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_5 = kgr_model4_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_6 = kgr_model5_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  
  actual_test = c()
  
  for (j in 1:12){
    actual_j = actual %>% filter(month == j) %>% select(deaths)
    est_lambda = mean(actual_j$deaths)
    
    if(est_lambda == 0){
      est_lambda = est_lambda+0.0001
    }
    
    actual_test = c(actual_test,as.numeric(est_lambda))
  }
  
  actual_test = c(actual_test[5:12],actual_test)

  pm_1_test = pm_1$mean[50:69]
  pm_2_test = pm_2$mean[50:69]
  pm_3_test = pm_3$mean[50:69]
  pm_4_test = pm_4$mean[50:69]
  pm_5_test = pm_5$mean[50:69]
  pm_6_test = pm_6$mean[50:69]

  actual_test_mean = mean(actual_test)
  
  values1 = c()
  values2 = c()
  values3 = c()
  values4 = c()
  values5 = c()
  values6 = c()
  
  for (j in 2:length(actual_test)){
    error1 = (abs(actual_test[j] - pm_1_test[j])) / (abs(actual_test[j] - actual_test[j-1])) / (length(actual_test)-1)
    values1 = c(values1,error1)
    
    error2 = (abs(actual_test[j] - pm_2_test[j])) / (abs(actual_test[j] - actual_test[j-1])) / (length(actual_test)-1)
    values2 = c(values2,error2)
    
    error3 = (abs(actual_test[j] - pm_3_test[j])) / (abs(actual_test[j] - actual_test[j-1])) / (length(actual_test)-1)
    values3 = c(values3,error3)
    
    error4 = (abs(actual_test[j] - pm_4_test[j])) / (abs(actual_test[j] - actual_test[j-1])) / (length(actual_test)-1)
    values4 = c(values4,error4)
    
    error5 = (abs(actual_test[j] - pm_5_test[j])) / (abs(actual_test[j] - actual_test[j-1])) / (length(actual_test)-1)
    values5 = c(values5,error5)
    
    error6 = (abs(actual_test[j] - pm_6_test[j])) / (abs(actual_test[j] - actual_test[j-1])) / (length(actual_test)-1)
    values6 = c(values6,error6)
  }
  
  mase1 = mean(values1)
  mase2 = mean(values2)
  mase3 = mean(values3)
  mase4 = mean(values4)
  mase5 = mean(values5)
  mase6 = mean(values6)
  
  MASE_table[i,] = c(mase1,mase2,mase3,mase4,mase5,mase6)
  # MASE_table[i,] = MASE_table[,i] / actual_test_mean
}

#Table 3: MASE on test dataset

MASE_table = data.frame(MASE_table)

rownames(MASE_table) = clusterlabels$counties
colnames(MASE_table) = c("BYM model","Proposed KGR model 1","Proposed KGR model 2","Proposed KGR model 3","Proposed KGR model 4","Proposed KGR model 5")

MASE_table
```

Mean absolute percentage error (MAPE), also known as mean absolute percentage deviation (MAPD), is a measure of prediction accuracy of a forecasting method in statistics, expressing accuracy as a ratio

MAPE = $\frac{100}{n} \sum_{t=1}^n |\frac{\hat \lambda_{t+h}^{obs} - \hat \lambda_{t+h}}{\hat \lambda_{t+h}^{obs}}|$

```{r}
MAPE_table = matrix(nrow=num_counties,ncol=6)

for (i in 1:num_counties){
  actual = inla_dataset %>% filter(county_id == i)  %>% data.frame()

  pm_1 = ref_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_2 = kgr_model1_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_3 = kgr_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_4 = kgr_model3_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_5 = kgr_model4_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_6 = kgr_model5_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>%
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()

  actual_test = c()
  
  for (j in 1:12){
    actual_j = actual %>% filter(month == j) %>% select(deaths)
    est_lambda = mean(actual_j$deaths)
    
    if(est_lambda == 0){
      est_lambda = est_lambda+0.0001
    }
    
    actual_test = c(actual_test,as.numeric(est_lambda))
  }
  
  actual_test = c(actual_test[5:12],actual_test)
  
  pm_1_test = pm_1[50:69,]
  pm_2_test = pm_2[50:69,]
  pm_3_test = pm_3[50:69,]
  pm_4_test = pm_4[50:69,]
  pm_5_test = pm_5[50:69,]
  pm_6_test = pm_6[50:69,]

  actual_test_mean = mean(actual_test)

  mape1 = mean(abs((actual_test - pm_1_test$mean)/actual_test))
  mape2 = mean(abs((actual_test - pm_2_test$mean)/actual_test))
  mape3 = mean(abs((actual_test - pm_3_test$mean)/actual_test))
  mape4 = mean(abs((actual_test - pm_4_test$mean)/actual_test))
  mape5 = mean(abs((actual_test - pm_5_test$mean)/actual_test))
  mape6 = mean(abs((actual_test - pm_6_test$mean)/actual_test))
  
  MAPE_table[i,] = c(mape1,mape2,mape3,mape4,mape5,mape6)
  # MAPE_table[i,] = MAPE_table[,i] / actual_test_mean
}

#Table 2: RMSE on test dataset

MAPE_table = data.frame(MAPE_table)

rownames(MAPE_table) = clusterlabels$counties
colnames(MAPE_table) = c("BYM model","Proposed KGR model 1","Proposed KGR model 2","Proposed KGR model 3","Proposed KGR model 4","Proposed KGR model 5")

MAPE_table
```

```{r}
#Overall fit
test_RMSE_table = matrix(nrow=num_counties,ncol=6)

for (i in 1:num_counties){
  actual = inla_dataset %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% data.frame()

  pm_1 = ref_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_2 = kgr_model1_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_3 = kgr_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_4 = kgr_model3_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_5 = kgr_model4_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()
  pm_6 = kgr_model5_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(county_id == i) %>% 
    select(mean,sd) %>% data.frame()

  actual_test = c()
  
  for (j in 1:12){
    actual_j = actual %>% filter(month == j) %>% select(deaths)
    est_lambda = mean(actual_j$deaths)
    
    actual_test = c(actual_test,as.numeric(est_lambda))
  }
  
  actual_test = c(actual_test[5:12],actual_test)
  
  pm_1_test = pm_1[50:69,]
  pm_2_test = pm_2[50:69,]
  pm_3_test = pm_3[50:69,]
  pm_4_test = pm_4[50:69,]
  pm_5_test = pm_5[50:69,]
  pm_6_test = pm_6[50:69,]

  actual_test_mean = mean(actual_test)

  test_rmse1 = sqrt(mean((actual_test - pm_1_test$mean)^2))
  test_rmse2 = sqrt(mean((actual_test - pm_2_test$mean)^2))
  test_rmse3 = sqrt(mean((actual_test - pm_3_test$mean)^2))
  test_rmse4 = sqrt(mean((actual_test - pm_4_test$mean)^2))
  test_rmse5 = sqrt(mean((actual_test - pm_5_test$mean)^2))
  test_rmse6 = sqrt(mean((actual_test - pm_6_test$mean)^2))
  
  test_RMSE_table[i,] = c(test_rmse1,test_rmse2,test_rmse3,test_rmse4,test_rmse5,test_rmse6)
  test_RMSE_table[i,] = test_RMSE_table[i,] / actual_test_mean
}

#Table 2: RMSE on test dataset

test_RMSE_table = data.frame(test_RMSE_table)

rownames(test_RMSE_table) = clusterlabels$counties
colnames(test_RMSE_table) = c("BYM model","Proposed KGR model 1","Proposed KGR model 2",
                              "Proposed KGR model 3","Proposed KGR model 4","Proposed KGR model 5")

test_RMSE_table
```

# Comparing coverage rates of credible intervals produced by each model

```{r}
coverage = rep(0,6)

true_values = inla_dataset$deaths
models_fvs = list(ref_model2_outfit$fitted_values,
                  kgr_model1_outfit$fitted_values,
                  kgr_model2_outfit$fitted_values,
                  kgr_model3_outfit$fitted_values,
                  kgr_model4_outfit$fitted_values,
                  kgr_model5_outfit$fitted_values)

# true_values = inla_test_dataset$deaths
# 
# r2_fvs = ref_model2_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(date >= "2018-05-01")
# k1_fvs = kgr_model1_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(date >= "2018-05-01")
# k3_fvs = kgr_model3_outfit$fitted_values %>% filter(date >= "2018-05-01")
# 
# models_fvs = list(r2_fvs,k1_fvs,k3_fvs)

for (i in 1:6){
  lci = models_fvs[[i]] %>% select('0.025quant')
  uci = models_fvs[[i]] %>% select('0.975quant')
  
  captured = (true_values >= lci$'0.025quant' & true_values <= uci$'0.975quant')
  coverage[i] = sum(captured)/length(captured)
}

coverage = data.frame(coverage)
colnames(coverage) = "95% coverage"
rownames(coverage) = c("BYM model","Proposed KGR model 1","Proposed KGR model 2",
                       "Proposed KGR model 3","Proposed KGR model 4","Proposed KGR model 5")

coverage
```

# What is DRF? DRF models the expected value of potential outcomes Y as a function of the treatment level and covariates

Use models fit above as DRF - DRF is a convex function $f: \mathcal{Z} x \mathcal{X} \rightarrow \mathcal{Y}$ s.t. $Y(Z) = Y(I,Z,\textbf{X}) = f(Z,\textbf{X}) + \textbf{F]$

$$
E[Y|Z = z_{i,t}, \textbf{X} = \textbf{x}_{i,t}] = E[Y(z_{i,t}) | Z = z_{i,t}, \textbf{X} = \textbf{x}_{i,t}] = E[Y(z_{i,t}) | \textbf{X} = \textbf{x}_{i,t}] = E[f(z_{i,t},\textbf{X}) + \textbf{F}_{i,t} | \textbf{X} = \textbf{x}_{i,t}] = f(z_{i,t},\textbf{x}_{i,t})
$$

Counterfactual prediction: change treatment level Z while holding covariates X fixed -\> estimate all potential outcomes for a given individual OR fix Z while varying X -\> estimate how treatment responses vary with individual level characteristics

# ADRF: average of DRF over all individuals in the population

$E[Y(z)] = E\{ E[Y(z) | \textbf{X}] \} = E[f(z,\textbf{X})] = \int_{\mathcal{X}} f(z,\textbf{x}) dP{\textbf{X}}(x) = \mu(z)$

Estimate by choosing a set of treatment values Z and averaging the predicted potential outcomes over the empirical distribution of X i.e. for each value z, estimate predicted value from DRF for every subject i in the sample and average those together

For each value of Z, predict Y for each county i using X_i -\> average those Ys together -\> one Ybar for each Z -\> ADRF(z)

Calculate ADRF for each month -\> 12 ADRFs -\> average across months -\> final ADRF

Create separate datasets to feed into INLA for each value of Z

```{r,eval=FALSE}
causal_dataset = inla_dataset

z_grid = seq(0,max(causal_dataset$wildfire_pm25),length.out = 20)

monthly_avg_dataset = causal_dataset %>% group_by(county,month) %>% summarize(aqi_avg = mean(b3_AQI),lead_avg = mean(b3_lead),
co_avg = mean(b3_co),so2_avg = mean(b3_so2),no2_avg = mean(b3_no2),o3_avg = mean(b3_o3),pm10_avg = mean(b3_pm10),
pm25_avg = mean(b3_pm25),treatment = mean(b3_wildfire_pm25))

ADRF_data_list = list()

for(i in 1:length(z_grid)){
  monthly_avg_dataset$treatment = z_grid[i]
  monthly_avg_dataset$deaths = NA
  monthly_avg_dataset$date  = "2018-05-01" #put a random date insample as placeholder
  ADRF_data_list[[i]] = monthly_avg_dataset
}
```

Calculate ADRF(z) for each month separately -\> 12 ADRFs -\> avg across months

Attach training data to potential outcome (ADRF) data

```{r,eval=FALSE}
ADRF_month = function(Month,model_num){
  ADRF_table = data.frame(month=NA,treatment=NA,potential_outcome=NA)

  for(i in 1:length(ADRF_data_list)){
    test = ADRF_data_list[[i]]
    month_test = test %>% filter(month == Month)

    inla_addon = inla_train_dataset[,c("county","month","b3_AQI","b3_lead","b3_co","b3_so2","b3_no2","b3_o3","b3_pm10","b3_pm25","b3_wildfire_pm25","deaths","date")]

    colnames(month_test) = colnames(inla_addon)
    ADRF_data = rbind(inla_addon,month_test)

    ADRF_data = ADRF_data %>% left_join(county_ids, by = "county")

    #Add multiple intercept columns, one for each cluster 
    ADRF_data <- ADRF_data %>%
      mutate(
        Intercept1 = ifelse(cluster == 1, 1, NA),
        Intercept2 = ifelse(cluster == 2, 1, NA),
        Intercept3 = ifelse(cluster == 3, 1, NA),
        Intercept4 = ifelse(cluster == 4, 1, NA),
        Intercept5 = ifelse(cluster == 5, 1, NA)
      ) 

    ADRF_data$idx = 1:nrow(ADRF_data)
    
    if(model_num == 1){
        model_ADRF = ref_model2(dataset = ADRF_data,a_prec_prior = 1,
                                b_prec_prior = 5e-4,a_phi_prior = 1,b_phi_prior = 5e-4)
    } else if(model_num == 2){
        model_ADRF = kgr_model1(dataset = ADRF_data,rho_time_rbf = 536.519, 
                                rho_time_periodic = 5.707,sigma2_time = 2.773)
    } else if(model_num == 3){
        model_ADRF = kgr_model2(dataset = ADRF_data, rho_Z_rbf = 0.0020604943,
                                rho_Z_periodic = 0.0024283451, sigma2_Z = 3.941096)
    } else if(model_num == 4){
        model_ADRF = kgr_model3(dataset = ADRF_data,
                            rho_Z_rbf = 37801.48, 
                            rho_Z_periodic = 571512.56,
                            rho_time_rbf = 39.5276, 
                            rho_time_periodic = 380794.8,
                            sigma2 = 1.124888)
    } else if(model_num == 5){
        model_ADRF = kgr_model4(dataset = ADRF_data,
                            rho_Z_rbf = 0.0008842497,
                            rho_Z_periodic = 0.0012119621,
                            rho_time_rbf = 0.0037851329,
                            rho_time_periodic = 0.003501742,
                            sigma2_Z = 4.310359,
                            sigma2_time = 0.9296752)
    } else if(model_num == 6){
        model_ADRF = kgr_model5(dataset = ADRF_data, rho_AR_rbf = 1.019648e-04, 
                        rho_AR_periodic = 3.904021e-04, rho_DL_rbf = 6.926401e-04,
                        rho_DL_periodic = 1.378534e-05, rho_int_rbf = 1.494036e-04,
                        rho_int_periodic = 3.228066e-04, sigma2_AR = 5.567759e-01,
                        sigma2_DL = 5.955738e-01, sigma2_int = 2.712031, link=1)
    }


    ADRF_preds = tail(model_ADRF$fitted_values,58)

    ADRF_pred = mean(ADRF_preds$mean)
    ADRF_response = data.frame(month=Month,treatment=z_grid[i],potential_outcome=ADRF_pred)

    ADRF_table = rbind(ADRF_table,ADRF_response)
  }
  
ADRF_table = ADRF_table[-1,]

  return(ADRF_table)
}

ADRF_Jan_ref2 = ADRF_month(Month=1,model_num = 1)
ADRF_Jan_kgr2 = ADRF_month(Month=1,model_num = 3)
ADRF_Jan_kgr3 = ADRF_month(Month=1,model_num = 4)
ADRF_Jan_kgr4 = ADRF_month(Month=1,model_num = 5)


# ADRF_Jan_kgr1_nonlinear = ADRF_month(Month=1,model_num=2)
# ADRF_Jan_kgr5_nonlinear = ADRF_month(Month=1,model_num=6)
```

Calculate some ADRFs: 

```{r}
ADRF_Jan_ref2 = readRDS("ADRF_Jan_ref2.rds")
ADRF_Jan_kgr1_nonlinear = readRDS("ADRF_Jan_kgr1_nonlinear.rds")
ADRF_Jan_kgr2 = readRDS("ADRF_Jan_kgr2.rds")
ADRF_Jan_kgr3 = readRDS("ADRF_Jan_kgr3.rds")
ADRF_Jan_kgr4 = readRDS("ADRF_Jan_kgr4.rds")
ADRF_Jan_kgr5_nonlinear = readRDS("ADRF_1_kgr5.rds")

ggplot(ADRF_Jan_ref2,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
ggplot(ADRF_Jan_kgr1_nonlinear,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
ggplot(ADRF_Jan_kgr2,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
ggplot(ADRF_Jan_kgr3,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
ggplot(ADRF_Jan_kgr4,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
ggplot(ADRF_Jan_kgr5_nonlinear,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
```
Calculate ADRF for every month

```{r,eval=FALSE}
for (i in 2:12){
  ref_model = ADRF_month(Month=i,model_num=1)
  kgr5 = ADRF_month(Month=i,model_num=6)
  
  file1 = sprintf("ADRF_%d_ref2.rds",i)
  file2 = sprintf("ADRF_%d_kgr5.rds",i)

  
  saveRDS(ref_model,file1)
  saveRDS(kgr5,file2)
}

# ADRF_Feb = test

# test = readRDS("ADRF_Jan_kgr1.rds")
# 
# plot = ggplot(ADRF_table,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
# plot
```

Average ADRF for each month into a single ADRF for each model 

```{r}
ADRF_overall = function(model_num,print=TRUE){
  potential_outcomes = rep(1,20)
  
  for (i in 1:12){
    if(model_num == 1){
      
      file = sprintf("ADRF_%d_ref2.rds",i) 
      ADRF_month = readRDS(file)
      potential_outcomes = cbind(potential_outcomes,ADRF_month$potential_outcome)
      
    } else if(model_num == 2){
        
      file = sprintf("ADRF_%d_kgr1.rds",i) 
      ADRF_month = readRDS(file)
      potential_outcomes = cbind(potential_outcomes,ADRF_month$potential_outcome)
      
    } else if(model_num == 3){
      
      file = sprintf("ADRF_%d_kgr2.rds",i) 
      ADRF_month = readRDS(file)
      potential_outcomes = cbind(potential_outcomes,ADRF_month$potential_outcome)  
      
    } else if(model_num == 4){
      
      file = sprintf("ADRF_%d_kgr3.rds",i) 
      ADRF_month = readRDS(file)
      potential_outcomes = cbind(potential_outcomes,ADRF_month$potential_outcome)  
      
    } else if(model_num == 5){
      
      file = sprintf("ADRF_%d_kgr4.rds",i) 
      ADRF_month = readRDS(file)
      potential_outcomes = cbind(potential_outcomes,ADRF_month$potential_outcome)  
      
    } else if(model_num == 6){
      
      file = sprintf("ADRF_%d_kgr5.rds",i) 
      ADRF_month = readRDS(file)
      potential_outcomes = cbind(potential_outcomes,ADRF_month$potential_outcome)  
      
    }
  }
  
  potential_outcomes = potential_outcomes[,-1]
  EYz = apply(potential_outcomes,1,mean)
  ADRF = data.frame(z_grid,EYz)
  colnames(ADRF) = c("treatment","potential_outcome")
  
  if(print == TRUE){
    ADRF_plot = ggplot(ADRF,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
    return(ADRF_plot)
  } else{
    return(ADRF)
  }
}

ADRF_overall(model_num=6)
```


```{r,eval=FALSE}
ADRF_Jan = readRDS("ADRF_1_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Feb = readRDS("ADRF_2_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Mar = readRDS("ADRF_3_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Apr = readRDS("ADRF_4_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_May = readRDS("ADRF_5_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Jun = readRDS("ADRF_6_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Jul = readRDS("ADRF_7_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Aug = readRDS("ADRF_8_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Sep = readRDS("ADRF_9_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Oct = readRDS("ADRF_10_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Nov = readRDS("ADRF_11_kgr5.rds")
ADRF_Dec = readRDS("ADRF_12_kgr5.rds")


ADRF_kgr5 = ADRF_Jan$treatment
EYz = cbind(ADRF_Jan$potential_outcome,ADRF_Feb$potential_outcome,ADRF_Mar$potential_outcome,
            ADRF_Apr$potential_outcome,ADRF_May$potential_outcome,ADRF_Jun$potential_outcome,
            ADRF_Jul$potential_outcome,ADRF_Aug$potential_outcome,ADRF_Sep$potential_outcome,
            ADRF_Oct$potential_outcome,ADRF_Nov$potential_outcome,ADRF_Dec$potential_outcome)
EYz = apply(EYz,1,mean)
ADRF_kgr5 = data.frame(ADRF_kgr5,EYz)
colnames(ADRF_kgr5) = c("treatment","potential_outcome")

ggplot(ADRF_kgr5,aes(x=treatment,y=potential_outcome)) + geom_line()
```





ADRF function prototype:

```{r,eval=FALSE}
test = ADRF_data_list[[1]]
Jan_test = test %>% filter(month == 1)

inla_addon = inla_train_dataset[,c("county","month","b3_AQI","b3_lead","b3_co","b3_so2","b3_no2","b3_o3","b3_pm10","b3_pm25","b3_wildfire_pm25","deaths","date")]

colnames(Jan_test) = colnames(inla_addon)
ADRF_data = rbind(inla_addon,Jan_test)

ADRF_data = ADRF_data %>% left_join(county_ids, by = "county")

#Add multiple intercept columns, one for each cluster 
ADRF_data <- ADRF_data %>%
  mutate(
    Intercept1 = ifelse(cluster == 1, 1, NA),
    Intercept2 = ifelse(cluster == 2, 1, NA),
    Intercept3 = ifelse(cluster == 3, 1, NA),
    Intercept4 = ifelse(cluster == 4, 1, NA),
    Intercept5 = ifelse(cluster == 5, 1, NA)
  ) 

ADRF_data$idx = 1:nrow(ADRF_data)
```

Fit INLA model -\> avg fitted values for each county together -\> perform for each month

```{r,eval=FALSE}
ref_model2_ADRF = ref_model2(dataset = ADRF_data,a_prec_prior = 1,b_prec_prior = 5e-4,a_phi_prior = 1,b_phi_prior = 5e-4)

kgr_model1_ADRF = kgr_model1(dataset = ADRF_data,rho_time_rbf = 536.519, rho_time_periodic = 5.707,sigma2_time = 2.773)

kgr_model3_ADRF = kgr_model3(dataset = ADRF_data, 
                            rho_EPA_rbf = 37801.48, 
                            rho_EPA_periodic = 571512.56,
                            rho_time_rbf = 39.5276, 
                            rho_time_periodic = 380794.8,
                            sigma2 = 1.124888)


ADRF_preds = tail(kgr_model3_ADRF$fitted_values,58)
ADRF_pred = mean(ADRF_preds$mean)
ADRF_response = data.frame(month=1,treatment=0,potential_outcome=ADRF_pred)
```

# AGTE: evaluate ADRF at two levels of treatment for which we want to compare and take the difference

$\tau_{AGTE}(z,z_0) := E[Y(z) - Y(z_0)]$

Estimate by evaluating ADRF at two levels of treatment that we are interested in (if z=0 then we are looking at AGTET) -\> take the difference

Ex: ADRF(100) - ADRF(0)

$\tau_{AGTET}(Z,0) := E[Y(Z) - Y(0) | Z > 0]$

Holding covariate values constant and making same comparison gives us CAGTE -\> comparing potential outcomes for different individuals (values of X) but same treatment level allows us to estimate heterogeneity in treatment effects

$\tau_{CAGTE}(z,z_0,\textbf{x}) := E[Y(z) - Y(z_0) | \textbf{X} = \textbf{x}]$

CAGTE(z,0,X) = ADRF(z\|x) - ADRF(0\|x)

CAGTE1: Fix X to be mean of air pollutant confounders and a fixed month e.g. June

CAGTE2: Fix X to be mode of air pollutant confounders and a fixed month e.g. June
